Package com.audiodo.aspen

Class IAncCapability

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.audiodo.aspen.IObservableProperty
      • com.audiodo.aspen.IAncCapability
  • public class IAncCapability
extends IObservableProperty
    The ANC capability

    This class is an interface to the ANC capability available on a DeviceService.
    It can be used to set a headset's ANC on, off, and in transparency mode, as well as
    getting the current state.

    The caller should implement a subclass of IPropertyObserver to get the asynchronous
    result of some of the method calls in this class.

      The event for when an audio device's ANC state has been set

      When set_anc_mode() has been successfully completed, this event is sent to
      the IPropertyObserver property_changed().

      • setAncMode

        public boolean setAncMode​(AncMode anc_mode)
        Set ANC mode on the audio device

        This method sets the ANC on the connected remote audio device to the desired state.

        A successful operation will result in an event in the observer's propertyChanged()
        method. The event that matches this call is get_observable_event_set_anc_mode(),
        and the subject passed contains no additional information (an empty string).

        Initial errors are communicated through the boolean return value, asynchronous errors
        will result in the device session being terminated, and the user will have to determine
        proper cause of action.

        NOTE: If called when the corresponding Aspen state already is as expected, the method
        will return true and also send the matching event; the rationale is that if the caller
        wants to do this action, there might be follow-up logic that the caller expects to be
        done in the event handler that wouldn't be executed if we just silently returned.

        If this method is called while there is an ongoing request through the AncCapability,
        it will return false until that request is processed. Listen to the events to know
        when a request is done.

        Parameters:
        anc_mode - The AncMode to set on the audio device
        Returns:
        True if the initial handling went well, false if something immediately went wrong.

      • getAncMode

        public AncMode getAncMode()
        Get the ANC mode

        This method returns the current ANC state of the audio device, or – if the audio device has
        disconnected – the last known state.

        Returns:
        The anc mode of the audio device.

      • ancModeInSync

        public boolean ancModeInSync()
        Check if remote audio device status is up to date

        This method allows the Aspen user to check if the remote audio device status is in sync,
        i.e. whether the ANC state is up to date. When disconnected, get_anc_mode() will return
        the status from when the audio device was last connected.

        When not in sync, that means that the current information might or might not
        properly reflect the status in the audio device – we just don't know, and the status
        we have is what we last knew to be true.

        Returns:
        True if the remote audio device status is in sync, false if not in sync.

      • isModeSupported

        public boolean isModeSupported​(AncMode mode)
        Check if the anc mode is supported

        Parameters:
        mode - The anc mode to check whether it is supported or not.

        Returns:
        Returns a boolean specifying whether the mode is supported or not.

      • getObservableEventSetAncMode

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventSetAncMode()
        The event for when an audio device's ANC state has been set

        When set_anc_mode() has been successfully completed, this event is sent to
        the IPropertyObserver property_changed().

        This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies the event.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventReceivedAncMode

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventReceivedAncMode()
        The event for when an audio device's ANC state has been received from the headset

        When AncCapability has connected to a headset with Audiodo ANC support, the device's
        ANC state is requested. Upon successful reception of the ANC state, this event is
        sent to the IPropertyObserver property_changed().

        This method can be used to get the string constant that identifies the event.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.
